repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
The decompressor is tiny enough to become part of the modules, thus making them stand-alone despite any potentially unavailable stdlib modules.

See discussion in https://github.com/cython/cython/pull/7546

comment_data.body: In principle I think you need to include stdint.h for uint8_t - it doesn't look to be part of the headers that Python.h includes automatically. I suspect it usually ends up included indirectly of course, but it's not guaranteed
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -135,8 +143,85 @@ static PyObject *__Pyx_DecompressString(const char *s, Py_ssize_t length, int al
     Py_XDECREF(module);
     Py_DECREF(methodname);
     return NULL;
+#endif
+}
+
+
+//////////////////// DecompressString_LZSS.proto ////////////////////
+// TODO: Move to the shared module if we can import that before initialising the strings.
+
+static PyObject *__Pyx_DecompressString_LZSS(const char *s, size_t compressed_length, size_t uncompressed_length); /*proto*/
+
+//////////////////// DecompressString_LZSS ////////////////////
+
+#ifndef __Pyx_DecompressString_LZSS_UNUSED
+CYTHON_UNUSED
+static CYTHON_SMALL_CODE size_t __pyx_lzss_decompress(const uint8_t *src, uint8_t *dst, size_t dst_len) {

comment_data.body:
We include it in `ModuleSetupCode.c`, so it's already there.
https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/d0835eabed53d79134a17959e49e359c19aa31c2/Cython/Utility/ModuleSetupCode.c#L547

Arguably, that's a little fragile because the usage there seems local and it's easy to accidentally remove the include when changing the code that seems to use it, but OTOH, it's such a basic header these days that I doubt that we'd consider removing the include without a bit of looking around if we depend on it elsewhere. I can add a comment that would make the dependency easier to spot.

comment_data.body: Fair enough - I did a `git grep` for `stdint` but missed it in ModuleSetupCode.c

commit.message:
Implement LZSS-like compression as default string compression.
The decompressor is tiny enough to become part of the modules, thus making them stand-alone despite any potentially unavailable stdlib modules.

See discussion in https://github.com/cython/cython/pull/7546
Fix and test storage of close matches.
Mark compression functions as potentially unused and suggest compiler optimisation for small size.
Fix C++ compilation.
Fix error output in test.
Minor test cleanup.
Fix test in Py3.14+, which dedents docstrings.
Update documentation.
Fix test in GraalPy.
Set CYTHON_COMPRESS_STRINGS as late as possible and disable default compression if LZSS doesn't compress enough.
Exclude compressed data that is worse than LZSS.
Only load utility code when it's used, not ahead of time.
Add a comment that the implementation depends on stdint.h.
Improve variable naming and add comments.
Save a few more instruction bytes.
Keep lazy-matching from preventing to collapse repetitive long sequences just because they are not finished at the current position yet.
Assert specific compression size in test.
Fix C++ compilation.
Fix "index out of bounds" in lazy matching.
Add missing C include.

title: Implement LZSS-like compression as default string compression
